text: Supporting\*Information\*
SI\*Materials\*and\*Methods

TS\*Structure.\*\*The!TS!structure!was!taken!from!a!previously!published!ab#initio!calculation! (1).!!To!assist!with!defining!geometric!constraints!in!the!calculations,!a!pseudoatom!(PSA)! with!no!charge!and!a!negligible!radius!was!added!0.01!Å!from!the!acidic!proton!in!the!plane! of!the!TS.!

Scaffold\* Selection.! ! For! the! initial! design,! the! xylanase! from! the! thermophilic! fungus! T. aurantiacus!(TAX)!was!selected!as!the!scaffold.!!The!crystal!structure!(PDB!code:!1GOR)!is! 1.7!Å!resolution!with!xylobiose!bound!in! the!active!site!(2).!!The!PDB!structure!was!used! without!minimization,!and!hydrogens!were!added!with!Molprobity!(3).!!Later!designs!used! the!native!binding!pockets!of!T.#maritima!imidazoleglycerolphosphate!synthase!(PDB!code: 1THF)!(4) and!S.#solfataricus!indoleT3Tglycerolphosphate!synthase!(PDB!code:!1A53)!(5)!as! scaffolds.

Gene\* Synthesis\* and\* Cloning.! ! The! gene! for! the! wildTtype! scaffold! was! backTtranslated! from! the! protein! sequence! using! the! codon! usage! bias! of! Escherichia# coli! in! DNA! 2.0! (6).! The! DNA! sequence! for! a! Factor! Xa! cleavage! site! and! sixThistidine! purification! tag! were! added!to!the! $3 ^ { \prime }$ !end!of!the!gene.!!Overlapping!oligonucleotides!spanning!the!gene!sequence! and! flanking!primers!were!designed!using!the!Assembly!PCR!Oligo!Maker!web!server!(7).! The! fullTlength!gene!was! constructed! by! recursive! PCR! using!a!method! based! on!the! one described! by! Stemmer! and! coworkers! (8).! ! After! amplification,! the! fullTlength! gene! was! inserted!into!a!pET11a!vector!(Novagen)!using!BamHI!and!NdeI.

Multiple\*Protein\*Expression\*and\*Purification\*Protocols.\*\*To!confirm!the!activity!of!HGT3! (HGT2/S265T),!independent!expression!and!purification!schemes!were!carried!out!at! two! different!laboratories.!!(i)!All!the!designed!enzymes!and!their!variants!were!expressed!and! purified! in! the! Division! of! Chemistry! and! Chemical! Engineering,! California! Institute! of! Technology,!Pasadena,!CA! using!protocol! 1.! !(ii)!HGT3!and!point!mutants!were!expressed! and! purified! using! independent! protocols! in! the! Laboratory! of! Organic! Chemistry,! ETH! Zurich,!Zurich,!Switzerland!using!protocol!2.!!Comparable!catalytic!activity!was!observed!in! both!labs.

Protein\*Expression\*and\*Purification:\*\*Protocol\*1.\*\*HGT2, HGT3, 1A53T1,!1A53T2,!1A53T3,! 1THFT1,!1THFT2 and!all!variants!were!expressed!in!BLT21!(DE3)!Escherichia coli!cells!in!LBT Amp.!!A!single!colony!was!used!to!inoculate!overnight!cultures!of!LBTAmp,!grown!at! $3 7 ~ ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ .! One! liter! of! LB! medium! supplemented! with! $1 0 0 \ \mathrm { \ m g / L }$ ! of! ampicillin! (LBTAmp)! was! inoculated!with! $3 0 ~ \mathrm { m L }$ !of!the!overnight!culture.!!The!cells!were!grown!with!shaking!at! $3 7 ~ ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ until! $\mathrm { O D } _ { 6 0 0 } \sim 0 . 3$ ; the! temperature!was!then! reduced! to! $1 8 ~ ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ ! until! $\mathrm { O D } _ { 6 0 0 } \sim 0 . 6$ .! ! Protein! expression! was! induced! with! $1 \ \mathrm { m M }$ ! isopropyl! $\beta$ TDT1Tthiogalactopyranoside! (IPTG)! and! carried!out!at! $1 8 ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ !for! $1 2 { \cdot } 1 8 \mathrm { h }$ .!!Cells!were!harvested!by!centrifugation!and!resuspended!in! $3 0 ~ \mathrm { m L }$ !lysis!buffer ( $2 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !Tris! $\mathrm { p H } 7 . 4$ ,! $3 0 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !NaCl)!containing! $1 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !imidazole;! $1 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ $\mathrm { M g C l } _ { 2 } ,$ ,! ribonuclease! A,! and! deoxyribonuclease! I! were! added! and! the! cells! were! lysed! mechanically!with!an!EmulsiflexTC5!(Avestin).!!The!soluble!fraction!was!incubated!for! ${ \sim } 1 \mathrm { ~ h ~ }$ with! $1 \textrm { m L }$ ! NiTNTA! resin! (Qiagen)! preTequilibrated! with! 10! bed! volumes! of! lysis! buffer! containing! $1 0 \mathrm { \ m M }$ imidazole.! ! The! resin! was! washed! in! a! gravity! column! with! 10! bed!

volumes! of! buffer! 2! (lysis! buffer! with! $2 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ ! imidazole)! and! purified! protein! was! eluted! with! 3! bed! volumes! of! buffer! 3! (lysis! buffer! with! $2 5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ ! imidazole).! ! The! eluate! was! concentrated! using! Amicon! centrifugal! concentrators! (Millipore)! and! the! buffer! was! exchanged!to! $5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !sodium!citrate,! $1 5 0 \ \mathrm { m M } \ \mathrm { N a C l } \ \mathrm { p H } \ 5 . 5$ !(for!1GORTbased!designs)!or!25! mM! HEPES,! $1 0 0 \ \mathrm { m M }$ ! NaCl! pH! 7.25! (for! 1THFT! and! 1A53Tbased! designs)! using! PDT10! desalting! columns! (GE).! ! Protein! purity! was! confirmed! by! SDSTPAGE. The! molecular! weights! of! all! proteins! were! confirmed! by! electrospray! ionization! mass! spectrometry. Protein!concentrations!were!determined!by!measuring!the!absorbance!at! $2 8 0 \mathrm { n m }$ !using!the calculated!extinction!coefficient! $\left( \varepsilon = 5 7 , 4 1 0 \mathrm { M } ^ { - 1 } \mathrm { c m } ^ { - 1 } \right)$ .!

Protein\*Expression\*and\*Purification:\*Protocol\*2. Here,!protein!expression!was!carried! out!as!above!except!that!cultures!contained! $1 5 0 ~ \mathrm { m g / L }$ !ampicillin,!expression!cultures!were! inoculated!with!the!overnight!culture!at!a!ratio!of!1:100,!and!induction!was!carried!out!for! $2 4 \mathrm { ~ h ~ }$ .! ! After! $2 4 \ \mathrm { h }$ ,! the! cells! were! harvested! and! resuspended! in! sonication! buffer! ( $2 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ Tris! $\mathrm { p H } ~ 7 . 4$ ,! $3 0 0 \ \mathrm { m M \ N a C l } )$ ! containing! $1 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ ! imidazole.! ! Cell! lysis! was! achieved! by! the! addition!of! $1 \mathrm { m g / m L }$ !lysozyme!and!subsequent!sonication.!The!soluble!fraction!was!applied! to!NiTNTA!slurry!(Qiagen),!washed!with! $2 0 \mathrm { m M }$ !imidazole!and!then!with! $3 2 . 5 \mathrm { m M }$ !imidazole! before!elution!with! $2 5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !imidazole!in!sonication!buffer.!!The!protein!was!dialyzed!into! $2 0 \ \mathrm { \ m M }$ ! Tris,! $2 0 \ \mathrm { \ m M }$ ! NaCl! $\mathrm { ( p H ~ 8 . 0 ) }$ ! for! $1 6 \mathrm { ~ h ~ }$ ! and! then! purified! by! anion! exchange! chromatography! (MonoQ! column,! GE! Healthcare)! in! the! same! buffer,! eluting! with! a! salt! gradient! $2 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !to! $1 , 0 0 0 \ \mathrm { m M \ N a C l } ]$ .!!The!protein!was!concentrated!using!an!Amicon!UltraT 15!unit!(Millipore).!
5BNitrobenzisoxazole\* (5BNBZ).\* This! compound! was! prepared! according! to! literature! procedures!(9,!10).

CD\* Spectroscopy.\* FarTUV! spectra were! measured! at! $2 5 \ ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ ! using! an Aviv! 62DS spectropolarimeter! equipped! with! a! thermoelectric! temperature! controller! (Aviv! Associates,! Lakewood,! NJ).! ! Thermal! denaturations were! monitored! at! $2 2 2 \ \mathrm { n m }$ .! ! All! experiments!were!carried!out!in!a! $1 \mathrm { m M }$ !cuvette!containing! $1 0 \mu \mathrm { M }$ !protein!in! $2 5 \mathrm { m M }$ !HEPES! $\mathrm { p H } ~ 7 . 2 5$ ,! $1 0 0 \ \mathrm { n M \ N a C l }$ .! ! Apparent! midpoints! of! thermal! denaturation! $\left( \mathrm { T } _ { \mathrm { m } } \right)$ ! were! obtained! using! the! equation! of! Minor! and! Kim (11).! ! $\mathrm { T } _ { \mathrm { m } }$ ! values! should! be! considered!approximate! and!not!actual! thermodynamic!parameters,!as! the!denaturation!was!not!reversible!in!any! case!tested.

Mass\*Spectrometry.\* The!protein!sample!was!desalted!using!a! $\mathsf { C } _ { 4 }$ ! ZipTip! (Millipore)! and! measured!in! $5 0 \%$ !acetonitrile/ $0 . 2 \%$ ! formic! acid! $\mathrm { ( p H ~ 2 . 0 ) }$ !by!electrospray!ionization!mass! spectrometry! (ESITMS) on! a! QTTOF! Ultima! mass! spectrometer! (Waters). The! mass! spectrum!of!each!protein!was!deconvoluted!using!MaxEnt1!software.

Protein\*Crystallization\* and\*Crystallography.\* \*Crystals! of!HGT1! (expressed!and!purified! using!protocol!1)!were!obtained!at!room!temperature!from!a! $4 \mathrm { m L }$ !hanging!drop!containing! $5 ~ \mathrm { m g / m L }$ ! HGT1! in! buffer! containing! $0 . 8 \mathrm { ~ M ~ }$ ! potassiumTsodium! tartrate,! 0.1! M! Tris! $\mathrm { p H } 8 . 5$ ,! $0 . 5 \%$ !PEGTMME!5000.!!The!crystal!was!cryoTprotected!in!the!reservoir!solution!with! $1 5 \%$ ethylene! glycol.! ! Data! were! collected! using! a! MicroMaxT007HF! XTray! generator! with! a! RAXIS! $\mathrm { I V } { + } +$ ! detector! (Rigaku! Corp.)! ! All! data! were! processed! using! CrystalClear! (Rigaku! Corp.)!and!MOSFLM!(12).

Crystals! of! HGT2/NBT,! 1A53T2/NBT,! and! apo! 1A53T2! were! obtained! through! sittingT drop!vapor!diffusion!carried!out!at!room!temperature!with!a!protein!concentration!of!9.5! $\mathrm { { m g / m L } }$ .! ! CoTcrystallization! for! HGT2/NBT! and! 1A53T2/NBT! was! achieved! through! preT incubation!of!the!protein!with! $5 ~ \mathrm { m M } ~ 5$ Tnitrobenzotriazole!(5TNBT,!Ryan!Scientific)!prior!to! crystallization! trials.! ! A! $1 0 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ ! stock! solution! of! 5TNBT! was! prepared! in! DMSO! before! combining!with!the!protein.!!Reservoir!solutions! for!HGT2/NBT!(0.1!M!sodium!acetate!pH! 4.6,!2!M!ammonium!sulfate),!1A53T2/NBT!(0.1!M!sodium!citrate/citric!acid!pH!5.6,! $0 . 2 \mathrm { ~ M ~ }$ potassium!sodium!tartrate,! $2 \mathbb { M }$ !ammonium!sulfate),!and!apo!1A53T2!(0.1!M!BisTTris! $\mathrm { p H } 5 . 5$ ,! $0 . 2 ~ \mathrm { { M } }$ !ammonium!acetate,!and! $2 5 \%$ !PEG!3350)!were!combined!with!protein!in!a!1:1!ratio.! A! single! HGT2/NBT,! multiple! cubeTlike! 1A53T2/NBT,! and! several! plateTlike! apo! 1A53T2! crystals! developed! with! a! minimum! growth! time! of! one! month.! ! The! crystals! were! cryoT protected!with!paraffin!oil!and!shipped!to!the!Stanford!Synchrotron!Radiation!Lightsource,! beamline! 12T2! for! remote! data! collection.! ! Diffraction! data! were! processed! with! the! program!MOSFLM!using!the!interface!iMOSFLM!(12).

Data!were! scaled!using! the!program!SCALA!(13).!!Molecular! replacement!was!carried! out!with!PHASER!(13,!14).!!The!coordinates! for!Thermoascus#aurantiacus#xylanase! I!(PDB! code:!1GOR)!(2)!and!Sulfolobus#solfataricus#(PDB!code:!1A53)!(5)!were!modified!to!contain! alanine!at!all!point!mutations!in!the!designs!and!were!subsequently!used!as!the!molecular! replacement! starting! models! for! HGT2! and! 1A53T2,! respectively.! ! Model! building! was! carried!out!using!COOT!(15).!!The!structure!was!refined!using!REFMAC!(16)!and!PHENIX! (17).! Backbone! density! for! the! HGT2! structure! appeared! in! two! distinct! backbone! conformations!in!chain!B,!similar!to!the!dual!backbone!conformation!found!in!the!structure! of! red! fluorescent! protein! variant! FP611! (PDB! code:! 3E5T) (18).! ! The! apo! structure! of! 1A53T2! was! processed! with! a! twinning! fraction! of! 0.13! towards! the! end! of! refinement.! Crystallographic!data!statistics!are!summarized!in!Table!S2.

Kinetic\*Measurements.!!Assays!were!carried!out!with! $5 ~ { \mu \mathrm { M } }$ !protein!(purified!via!protocol! 1)!in! $2 5 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !HEPES,! $\mathrm { p H } 7 . 2 5$ ,! $1 0 0 \ \mathrm { m M \ N a C l }$ !at! $2 7 ~ ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ .!!Substrate!concentrations!between!16! $\mu \mathrm { M }$ !and! $1 \mathrm { m M }$ !were!used!and!the!acetonitrile!concentration!was!kept!constant!at! $2 \%$ !of!the! $1 ~ \mathrm { m L }$ !reaction!volume.!!Product!formation!was!monitored!at! $3 8 0 ~ \mathrm { n m }$ ! $[ \varepsilon = 1 5 , 8 0 0 ~ \mathrm { c m ^ { - 1 } M ^ { - 1 } } ]$ ! using! a! Shimadzu! UVT1601! spectrophotometer.! ! Initial! reaction! rates! were! determined from! the! linear! portion! of! the! reaction! progress! curve! and! were! corrected! for! the! initial! rate! contribution! for! the! buffer! catalyzed! (no! protein)! reaction.! ! Kaleidagraph! (Synergy! Software)! was! used! to! fit! the! data! to! the! MichaelisTMenten! equation! to! determine! the! kinetic!parameters! $k _ { c a t }$ !and! $K _ { m }$ :!

where! $\nu _ { O }$ ! is! the! initial! reaction! rate,! $[ E ] _ { T }$ !is! the! total!enzyme!concentration,!and! $[ S ] _ { 0 }$ ! is! the! initial!substrate!concentration.!!In!the!cases!where!the!enzyme!was!not!saturated!because! €of!limited!substrate!solubility,!the!data!were!fit!to!a!line!to!determine! $k _ { c a t } / K _ { m }$ .

Where!indicated!in!Table!1,!assays!were!carried!out!using! $1 ~ \mu \mathrm { M }$ !of!protein!purified!via! expression!protocol!2!in! $5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !phosphate!buffer! $\mathrm { ( p H ~ 7 . 0 ) }$ ,!containing! $1 0 0 \ \mathrm { m M \ N a C l }$ ! and! $2 \%$ ! acetonitrile.! ! Reactions! were! initiated! by! adding! different! amounts! of! 5TNBZ! in! acetonitrile! $ { \lbrack 2 5 \ \mu \mathrm { M } }$ !to! $1 . 2 5 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !final!concentration).!!Product!formation!was!monitored!at! $3 8 0 \mathrm { n m }$ !in!a!Lambda!35!UV/Vis!spectrometer!(PerkinElmer)!at! $2 7 ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ .

pHBRate\*Profile.!!For!the!pHTrate!profile,!reactions!were!initiated!by!addition!of!5TNBZ!(50! $\mu \mathrm { M }$ !final!concentration)!to!1!to! $4 \mu \mathrm { M }$ !protein!in!the!following!reaction!buffers:! $5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !buffer! salt![sodium!acetate! $\mathrm { ( p H < 6 . 0 ) }$ ),!sodium!phosphate $( 6 . 0 < \mathrm { p H } < 8 . 0 )$ ,!and!sodium!carbonate! $\mathrm { ( p H ~ > ~ 8 . 0 ) } ]$ ,! containing! $1 0 0 ~ \mathrm { \ m M }$ ! NaCl! and! $2 \%$ ! acetonitrile.! Product! formation! was! monitored! as! described! above.! The! $\mathrm { p H }$ ! of! all! reaction! solutions! was! confirmed! after! completion! of! the! reaction! using! a! pHTMeter! 526! (WissenschaftlichTTechnische! Werkstätten).!The!data!were!fit!to!the!equation:

$K _ { i }$ Determination.\* After! preincubation! of! $1 ~ \mu \mathrm { M }$ ! HGT3! with! varying! concentrations! of! 5Tnitrobenzotriazole! $( 0 . 0 0 1 - 1 0 0 0 \mu \mathrm { M }$ !final!concentrations)!in! $5 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ !acetate!buffer! $\left( \mathrm { p H } 5 \right)$ ! containing! $1 0 0 ~ \mathrm { m M }$ €! NaCl,! $2 \%$ ! acetonitrile,! and! $1 \%$ ! DMSO,! the! reactions! were! initiated! by! addition! of! 5TNBZ $( 2 5 0 ~ \mu \mathrm { M }$ ! final! concentration).! Product! formation! was! monitored! as described! above.! The! $I C _ { 5 0 }$ ! value! was! determined! by! curve! fitting! (HillTSlope! model,! $\nu _ { i }$ at infinite! inhibitor! concentration! was! set! to! zero)! and! subsequently! converted! into! the! corresponding! $K _ { i }$ value! using! the! ChengTPrusoff!equation! $K _ { i } = [ I C _ { 5 0 } ] / ( 1 + [ S ] / K _ { \mathrm { m } } )$ (19).! The! $K _ { m }$ ! value! was! determined! independently! in! the! same! buffer! and! under! identical! reaction! conditions!using!the!protocol!described!above.

MD\* Simulations.! ! MD! simulations! were! carried! out! for! 20! ns! for! each! enzymeTsubstrate! complex!at!NPT!conditions!(constant!number!of!particles,!pressure,!and!temperature)!with! a!pressure!of!1!bar!and!temperature!of! $3 0 0 ~ \mathrm { K }$ .!!The!TIP3P!explicit!solvent!model!(20)!was! used!to!solvate!the!protein!in!an!octahedralTshaped!volume,!ensuring!a!solvent!layer!of!at! least! $1 0 \mathrm { ~ \AA ~ }$ from! any! point! on! the! protein! surface.! ! Each! face! of! the! octahedral! box! is! connected!to!a!mirror!image!of!itself,!which!allows!the!system!to!be!treated!with!periodic! boundary! conditions.! ! This! ensures! equilibration! through! diffusion! while! the! number! of! particles!is!kept!constant.!!The!temperature!is!regulated!and!evenly!distributed!through!a! Langevin! equilibration! scheme.! ! The! 5TNBZ! substrate! was! parameterized! from! quantum! mechanical! calculations! in! order! to! be! treated! correctly! by! the! AMBER! force! field! (21).! Prior!to!production!MD,!the!substrate!was!superimposed!onto!the!ab initio!transition!state! in! the! active! site! of! the! computationally! designed! protein! model.! The! structure! of! the! resulting!proteinTsubstrate!complex!was!optimized!and!heated!to! $3 0 0 ~ \mathrm { K }$ in!six!50!ps!steps! for!a!total!of!300!ps!at!NVT!conditions,!and!then!equilibrated!at!NPT!conditions!for! $2 \mathrm { n } s$ .

The! structural! models! predicted! by! the! computational! design! algorithm! were! used! as! starting!structures.!!Substrate!parameters!were!generated!within!the!Antechamber!module! of!AMBER!10!(22) using! the!general!AMBER! force! field,!with!partial!charges!set! to! fit! the! electrostatic!potential!generated!at! $\mathrm { H F } / 6 – 3 1 \mathrm { G } ^ { * }$ !by!RESP!(23). !The!charges!were!calculated! according!to!the!MerzTSinghTKollman!scheme!(24,!25)!using!Gaussian!03!(26).

The! structures! were! immersed! in! a! truncated! octahedral! box! with! a! 10! Å! buffer! of! TIP3P! (20)! water! molecules,! resulting! in! the! addition! of! up! to! 16,000! solvent! molecules,! depending! on! the! scaffold.! ! The! systems! were! neutralized! by! addition! of! explicit! counter! ions.! ! All! subsequent! calculations! were! done! using! the! widely! tested! Stony Brook! modification! of! the! AMBER! 99! force! field! (27).! A! twoTstage! geometry! optimization! approach! was! utilized,! initially! minimizing! the! positions! of! water! molecules! and! ions,! followed!by!an!unrestrained!minimization!of!all!atoms.!!The!systems!were!heated!gently!in! six!steps!of!50!ps!from!0!to! $3 0 0 ~ \mathrm { K }$ !at!constant!volume!periodic!boundary!conditions.!!Weak!

harmonic! restraints! of! 30! kcal/mol! were! applied! to! the! solute,! and! the! Langevin! equilibration!scheme!was!used!to!control!and!equalize!the!temperature.!!The!time!step!was! kept! at! 1! fs! during! the! heating! stages,! allowing! potential! inhomogeneities! to! selfTadjust.! Each!system!was!then!equilibrated!for!2!ns!with!a!2!fs!time!step!at!a!constant!pressure!of!1! atm.! ! Water! molecules! were! triangulated! with! the! SHAKE! algorithm! such! that! the! angle! between! the! hydrogen! atoms! is! kept! fixed.! ! A! 20! ns! production! MD! simulation! was! performed!for!each!of!the!systems!(with!and!without!the!substrate!bound!to!the!active!site)! using!PMEMD!(28).!!Geometries!and!velocities!were!saved!every!100!steps! $( 0 . 2 { \mathrm { ~ p s ) } }$ ,!which! resulted! in! a! total! of! 10,000! and! 100,000! frames! from! each! production! run.! ! LongTrange! electrostatic! effects! were! modeled! using! the! particleTmeshTEwald! method! (29).! ! PostTMD! data extraction!and!analysis!were!performed!using!the!ptraj!module!of!AMBER!10!(22)!and! the!statistical!analysis!software!OriginPro8!(Origin!Lab).

QM\*Calculations.!!Theozyme!DFT!B3LYP/6T31G(d)!transition!state!(TS)!calculations!were! performed! on! a! model! system! that! consists! of! the! substrate,! acetate! as! the! general! base,! and!methanol!as!the!hydrogenTbond!donor.!The!distance!versus!angle!scatterTpoint!of!the! deprotonation!is!plotted!in!Figure!3C!and!3D.
Contact: Asp/Glu to H3\*

image_caption: Fig. S1. CD Characterization of HG-1 and HG-2. (A) Far UV wavelength scan. (B) Thermal denaturation.
Fig. S2. The active site of HG-1 (A) prior to MD, $( B )$ after MD on the 5-NBZ:HG-1 complex, and (C) after MD in the absence of 5-NBZ. All distance values are in Å.

Fig. S3. MD of HG-2 with 5-NBZ bound in an alternative orientation (O2). Distance-time plots and projected distance distributions for the contacts between (A) the acidic H of 5-NBZ and the Asp127 carboxylate oxgens, (B) the heterocycle oxygen of 5-NBZ and the Hs of Lys50, and (C) the nitro group oxygens of 5-NBZ and the OH of Ser265. $( D )$ A representative MD snapshot of the HG-2 active site with 5-NBZ bound in orientation O2. All distance values are in Å.
Fig. S4. (A) RMSD plot of the active site residues of HG-2. (B) Histogram of distance distribution between the carboxylate oxygens of D127 and the backbone NH of G82.
Fig. S5. Active site of HG-2. (A) Computational design model. (B) MD snapshot of state 1. (C) MD snapshot of state 2.

Fig. S6. pH rate profile of HG-2/S265T. HG-2/S265T exhibits a bell-shaped pH-rate profile with inflection points at $\mathsf { p H } 6 . 0$ and 7.8. The pH optimum of enzymatic activity is at $\mathsf { p H } 7 . 0$ . Experiments were performed in $5 0 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ acetate (pH 4.0-5.6), phosphate (pH 6.3-8.0), and carbonate (pH 9) buffer, each containing 100 mM NaCl.

Fig. S7. $I C _ { 5 0 }$ determination for 5-nitrobenzotriazole and HG-3. The assays were carried out with $2 5 0 ~ \mu \mathrm { M }$ 5-NBZ, $5 0 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ acetate $\mathsf { p H } ~ 5 . 0$ , $1 0 0 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ NaCl, $2 \%$ acetonitrile, $1 \%$ DMSO, $1 ~ \mu \mathsf { M }$ enzyme, at ${ } ^ { 2 7 } \ ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C }$ . In the curve-fit legend, m1 is the difference between $( v _ { 0 } / E ) _ { \mathrm { m i n } }$ and $( v _ { 0 } / E ) _ { \mathrm { m a x } }$ , $m 2$ is the $I C _ { 5 0 } ,$ , and $m 3$ is the Hill coefficient. In the absence of inhibitor, (HG-3) has an apparent $\mathsf { k } _ { \mathsf { c a t } } = 0 . 1 1 0 \pm 0 . 0 0 6 \ \mathsf { s } ^ { - 1 }$ and ${ \sf K } _ { \mathrm { m } } = 2 . 1 4 \pm 0 . 2 \mathrm { m N }$ under these assay conditions.

Fig. S8. Overlay of 1THF-based KE design models with KE07 design model (30). (A) 1THF-1 catalytic residues (purple sticks) are overlaid with KE07 catalytic residues (grey sticks). $( B )$ 1THF-2 catalytic residues (yellow sticks) are overlaid with KE07 catalytic residues (grey sticks).

Fig. S9. Overlay of 1A53-based KE design models with KE59 design model (30). (A) 1A53-2 catalytic residues (green sticks) are overlaid with KE59 catalytic residues (grey sticks). $( B )$ 1A53- catalytic residues (cyan sticks) are overlaid with KE59 catalytic residues (grey sticks).
Fig. S10. Michaelis-Menten plots of designed enzymes based on the scaffolds 1A53 and 1THF. Standard errors are calculated from three measurements.
